Thanks for the review. [~junping_du]
bq.  I think that could be helpful for trouble shooting. Isn't it? Also, do we need to close os which output stream? I haven't check if caller have similar logic. If we already have, we can skip here.

Yes, we have close it in client side. We do not need to do it on server side.

Uploaded patches for trunk and branch-2 for other comments

Thanks [~xgong] for updating the patch!
bq. Yes, we have close it in client side. We do not need to do it on server side.
I see. Just want to make sure no socket leaking in exception case.

Unit test failure is not related and get tracked in YARN-5934.
+1. Will commit it tomorrow if no further comments from others.
I have commit the patch to trunk and branch-2. Thanks [~xgong] for patch contribution!

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #11199 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/11199/])

Thanks for the review. [~junping_du]
bq.  I think that could be helpful for trouble shooting. Isn't it? Also, do we need to close os which output stream? I haven't check if caller have similar logic. If we already have, we can skip here.

Yes, we have close it in client side. We do not need to do it on server side.

Thanks [~xgong] for updating the patch!
bq. Yes, we have close it in client side. We do not need to do it on server side.
I see. Just want to make sure no socket leaking in exception case.

Unit test failure is not related and get tracked in YARN-5934.
+1. Will commit it tomorrow if no further comments from others.
I have commit the patch to trunk and branch-2. Thanks [~xgong] for patch contribution!

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #11199 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/11199/])
